_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies Montana as photo ID required as of 2023 (HB 176 took effect). Montana's primary is typically the first Tuesday after the third Monday in June; estimated 2026-06-02. Montana offers same-day voter registration only for municipal elections, not partisan primary elections — verify with SoS for 2026 cycle. 2026 registration deadlines estimated from Montana statute (30 days before for mail/online, Election Day for in-person non-partisan). TODO verify exact dates.

earlyVoting.notes: Montana offers absentee voting (in-person at county election offices) beginning 30 days before the election. All voters may request an absentee ballot without a reason. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

Absentee in-person voting available at county election offices starting 30 days before Election Day. Absentee ballots may be returned by mail or in person. Late registration (in-person at election office) may be available through noon the day before the election — check with your county.

Montana does not have traditional early voting polling places. Absentee in-person voting is available at county election offices starting approximately 30 days before the election. Any voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason.

acceptedIds: Montana driver's license or state ID card
Student photo ID from a Montana college or university (accompanied by proof of current enrollment or a current utility bill)
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Montana law prohibits photographing your marked ballot and showing it to others for purposes of vote-buying, but does not have a general ban on phones in polling places. Contact your county election office for local rules.
additionalRules: Montana has an open primary — any registered voter may vote in any party's primary.
Any voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee).
Late in-person registration may be available at county election offices through noon the day before the election — check with your county.
Photo ID is now required at the polls as of 2023 (HB 176). Previous rules requiring non-photo ID were changed.

eligibility: Any registered Montana voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by county election office by noon the day before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by county election office by 8:00 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county election office by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks alone do not count — ballot must be received.
